name: docs-site
comment:overrides: next@16.2.11 pins postcss 8.4.31 in its own nested tree, which is affected by
GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 (moderate: postcss < 8.5.10 fails to escape </style> when
stringifying a CSS AST). Our own top-level postcss is already 8.5.x; this
override lifts the nested copy to the patched line too, so no vulnerable
postcss is installed anywhere in the tree. Remove once Next ships a patched pin.
sharp is an optional dep of next (declared ^0.34.5, unchanged as of next@16.2.11)
and is affected by GHSA-f88m-g3jw-g9cj (high: inherited libvips CVE-2026-33327,
CVE-2026-33328, CVE-2026-35590, CVE-2026-35591). This site never invokes sharp:
next.config.mjs sets output:'export' + images.unoptimized:true. Override lifts it
to a patched line anyway so npm audit reports 0 vulnerabilities.
fast-uri (pulled in via ajv, itself a dep of fumadocs' schema tooling) is affected
by GHSA-v2hh-gcrm-f6hx (high: host confusion via a literal backslash authority
delimiter) below 3.1.4. Override lifts it to the patched line; stays within ajv's
comment:typescript: Held at ^6.0.3 (not the latest 7.x) deliberately: typescript@7 restructured to a
pure-ESM package and dropped lib/typescript.js, but next@16.2.11's TypeScript
detector (verify-typescript-setup.js) hard-codes an existsSync check for exactly
that file, so a genuinely-installed typescript@7.x reads as 'missing' and the
full source-read proof. Unpin once next ships a detector that resolves
